observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor avail the One-Time Cleansing of PPE Account Balances of Government Agencies as provided in COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020, particularly applying the prescribed procedures and disposition for missing and unaccounted PPE.
observations.managementAction: The MAO and MGSO committed to avail the One-Time Cleansing of PPE Account Balances in accordance with COA Circular No. 2020-006. The Municipality is still finalizing and verifying the list of missing and unaccounted PPE. The One-Time Cleansing had started last July 2024 and we will try to conclude the process by the end of CY 2025.
observations.reason: The GSO is still finalizing and verifying the list of missing and unaccounted PPE. One Time Cleansing had started last July 2024 and concluded the process is targeted by end of CY 2025.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to:

a. Determine the cause/s of the negative balance of the Net Assets/Equity of the
Municipality as of December 31, 2024, and thereafter, pass the necessary
accounting entry to adjust or correct the same; and

b. Henceforth, strictly adhere to the provisions of RA No. 7160, PD No. 1445,
MNGAS for LGUs and IPSAS regarding the accounting and utilization of the TF.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

a. Direct the Head of the Department or Office who has administrative control of
the fund concerned to ensure preparation of DV in every payment or settlement
of government payables/obligations, regardless of mode of payment; and

b. Henceforth, strictly adhere with the disbursement process and documentation
set forth in the MNGAS for LGUs, COA Circular No. 2006-002 and related
issuances, to avoid potential legal issues and recurrence of this observation.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and Chairman of the LSB:

a. Direct the LSB to include the amount of P1,398,230.27 in the CY 2025 SEF
Budget and present the line items therein by Programs, Activities and Projects
(PAPs), allotment class and per school following Item 5.2.2 of DepEd-DBM-DILG
JC No. 1, s. 2017 and then submit to the Audit Team the copy of the CY 2025
SEF Budget and the LSB Resolution/s approving the same, for verification and audit;
b. Henceforth, strictly adhere with the DepEd-DBM-DILG JC No. 1, s. 2017 and
succeeding issuances regarding the proper documentation, monitoring,
transparency and accountability in the allocation and utilization of SEF.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to:

a. Avail the procedures of the one-time cleansing of dormant accounts pursuant
to COA Circular No. 2023-008 if the dormant accounts are not covered by other
specific laws, rules and regulations prescribing their disposition, to finally
address the issues on dormancy; and

b. Prepare and submit to the Audit Team the SDAs on or before the 15th day of the
ensuing month in the form and following the instructions under Annexes 1 to 4
of COA Circular No. 2023-008.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the: a. Municipal Accountant and Municipal General Services Officer (MGSO) to report in the books of the Municipality and the RPCSP, respectively, all remaining semi-tangible items which meet the definition and recognition criteria of PPE but cost is below P50,000.00 as semi-expendable property, which as of December 31, 2023 totaled P2,648,060.00; and b. After effecting aforesaid changes, submit the updated Journal Entry and the revised RPCPPE of the Municipality to this Audit Team for further verification and audit.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal General Services Officer (MGSO) prepared the necessary accounting entries to remove the remaining semi-expendable properties included in the balance of PPE account at the same time the MGSO updated the RPCPPE and RPCSP to properly account the same. The copy of the JEV and the revised RPCPPE, as well as the JEVs, were submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a. Ensure that the APP of the Municipality for CY 2024 and succeeding years are in accordance with the requirements on the preparation of the APP under GPPB Circular No. 07-2015 and submit the same to the Audit Team as accompanying document to the Annual Budget; and b. Ensure 100 percent accomplishment of the procurement activities considering funds are available thereto and henceforth, strictly adhere to the provisions of the 2016 Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations (IRR) of Republic Act (RA) No. 9184 and related issuances regarding procurement and the preparation of relevant plans and documents.
observations.managementAction: The CY 2024 APP of the Municipality was revised in accordance with GPPB Circular No. 07-2015, indicating the schedule for each procurement activity, and was submitted to the Audit Team for verification. The Municipality significantly accomplished the procurement activities considering funds are available thereto and henceforth, strictly adhere to the provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 and related issuances regarding procurement and the preparation of relevant plans and documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to determine the causes of the negative balance of the Other Payables (2-99-99-990) account of the Municipality as at December 31, 2023 and prepare the necessary accounting entries to correct or adjust the same and submit the corresponding Journal Entry (JEV), supporting documents and the Subsidiary Ledger (SL) to the COA Audit Team for verification and audit and henceforth, strictly adhere to the provisions of IPSAS 1, COA Circular No. 2015-009 and the Manual in the accounting of the financial transactions and in keeping related records such as the SL.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant explained that the abnormal balance was due to wrongful entry in January 2021. A correcting entry has been prepared and submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) Focal Person to ensure 100 percent utilization of the GAD Fund considering funds are available thereto.
observations.managementAction: From the total of P12,156,700.00 approved GAD Budget, the agency’s utilization/expenditure is P7,682,529.63 or 63 percent of the total budget. The unutilized portion was considered in the succeeding budget preparation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
a. Officer-in-Charge (OIC) - Municipal Accountant and the MGSO to report in the
books of accounts of the Municipality and the RPCSP, respectively, all tangible
items which meet the definition and recognition criteria of PPE but cost is below
P50,000.00 as semi-expendable property, which as of December 31, 2023 totaled
P2,648,060.00; and
b. after effecting aforesaid changes, submit the pertinent JEV and the revised
RPCPPE of the Municipality to this Audit Team for further verification and audit.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. ensure that the APP of the Municipality for CY 2024 and succeeding years are in
accordance with the requirements on the preparation of the APP under GPPB
Circular No. 07-2015 and submit the same to the COA Audit Team as accompanying
document to the Annual Budget; and
b. ensure 100 percent accomplishment of procurement activities considering
funds are available thereto and henceforth, strictly adhere to the provisions of
the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 and related issuances regarding
procurement and the preparation of relevant plans and documents.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the OIC-Municipal Accountant
to determine the causes of the negative balance of the Other Payables (2-99-99-990)
account of the Municipality as at December 31, 2023 and prepare the necessary
accounting entries to correct or adjust the same and submit the corresponding
Journal Entry Voucher (JEV), supporting documents and the SL to the COA Audit
Team for verification and audit and henceforth, strictly adhere to the provisions of
IPSAS 1, COA Circular No. 2015-009 and the Manual in the accounting of the
financial transactions and in keeping related records such as the SL.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the GFPS Focal Person to ensure
100 percent utilization of the GAD Fund considering funds are available thereto.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a. direct the Officer-in-Charge (OIC) - General Services Officer and OIC - Municipal Accountant to exert all efforts to determine the missing and unserviceable properties and extent of liabilities for the missing and unserviceable properties that were not able to trace were requested for derecognition. b. avail the One-Time Cleansing of PPE Account Balances of Government Agencies as provided in Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020, particularly applying the prescribed procedures and disposition for missing and unaccounted PPE.
observations.managementAction: The General Services Officer located some properties listed as missing but found out that the properties are unserviceable and were included in the list of unserviceable. The Municipality committed to avail the One-Time Cleansing of PPE Account Balances of Government Agencies as provided in Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020, particularly applying the prescribed procedures and disposition for missing and unaccounted PPE.
observations.reason: The Municipality has yet to avail the One-Time Cleansing of PPE account

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a. require the Municipal Engineer and the Municipal Accountant to compute the liquidated damages equivalent to the delays of completion of the projects and demand from the concerned Contractors the payment thereof to avoid audit disallowance; b. direct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to include in the contract a provision for liquidated damages; and c. require the Municipal Engineer and the Municipal Accountant to strictly impose and deduct liquidated damages equivalent to the delays of the Contractors before final payment is made.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineer and the Accounting Office computed the liquidated damages equivalent to the delays of completion then the Engineering Office demanded the contractors the payment thereof. The BAC included a provision for liquidated damages in the contract. The Accounting Office is now double checking the date if the completion date of the project is within the agreed terms and if not liquidated damages are computed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to strictly implement the provisions of Sections 6.1 and 6.2 of Annex E of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 concerning retention money.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant now deducts retention money on progress payment until fifty percent of the value of the works as determined by the procuring entity. No further retention shall be made provided that the work is satisfactory done and on schedule.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: require the Municipal Treasurer and other concerned officials to prepare and submit the necessary requirements to enable the Municipality to include in its manual the automated payroll system the payment of the wages of its Job Order workers.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality required all the Job Order Employees to open their individual bank account to facilitate transfer of wages instead of cash payment.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Engineering Office to conduct preliminary inspection and submit punch-lists to contractors when they reach an accomplishment of 95 percent of the total contract amount to determine defects and thus, make necessary corrections, before proper turnover and acceptance of the projects to the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineering Office is now conducting preliminary inspection and submit punch list to the contractors.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor create a disposal team to facilitate the immediate disposal of the unserviceable properties, including the determination of the appropriate mode of disposal, appraisal of the same to prevent further deterioration and reduction of the properties’ economic value.
observations.managementAction: The disposal committee invited possible buyer of unserviceable properties after the assessment of cost of Appraisal Committee. The disposal was facilitated on January 2024.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council (MDRRMC) to identify and incorporate, under a separate caption, in the LDRRMFIP the projects and activities to be charged out of the unexpended previous years’ LDRRMF pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-002.
observations.managementAction: The LDRRMF in the Special Trust Fund was programmed and included in the Municipality’s 2023 LDRRMFIP.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a. require the Market Administrator to strictly enforce the provisions of the Municipal Market Code and to submit the List/Schedule of delinquent Lessees with accounts and with current unpaid rental fees as of December 31, 2022 to the Municipal Accountant for recording as Accounts Receivable; b. instruct the Municipal Accountant to record the necessary Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) based on the Schedule of Lessees with delinquent accounts and with current unpaid rental fees as of December 31, 2022; and c. instruct the Market Administrator and the Municipal Treasurer to consistently send demand/collection letters to delinquent tenants/lessees, and to recommend to the Market Committee the termination of contracts of those who continuously refuse to comply with the demand for their payments.
observations.managementAction: The Market Administrator submitted the List/Schedule of Lessees with delinquent accounts and with current unpaid rental fees to the OIC-Municipal Accountant as basis for recording. The OIC-Municipal Accountant had recorded the JEV based on the schedule given. The Market Administrator and the Municipal Treasurer committed to send demand letter to delinquent tenants/lessees, if any, with the provision that non-compliance with the said letter will result to termination of respective contract.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: b. direct the General Services Officer (GSO) to coordinate with the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Engineer in securing the documents needed as basis in recording and updating their respective PPE Property Cards (PC); and c. require the GSO to update all PCs and all records based on the latest RPCPPE.
observations.managementAction: The GSO together with Municipal Accountant and Municipal Engineer secured the documents needed as basis in recording and updating their respective PPE Property Cards (PC); and The GSO updated all PCs and all records based on the latest RPCPPE.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor create a disposal team to facilitate the immediate disposal of the unserviceable properties, including the determination of the appropriate mode of disposal, appraisal of the same to prevent further deterioration and reduction of the properties’ economic value.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor created a disposal team to facilitate the disposal of unserviceable properties. The disposal proceedings commenced in January 2024.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. re-establish an Inventory Committee which shall compose of adequate number of
members who shall be temporarily relieved of all their regular duties to devote
their full time to be able to complete the physical inventory in three months or
less, in compliance with COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020;
b. direct the Inventory Committee to conduct complete physical count of the
Municipality’s PPE according to their prepared Physical Inventory Plan and
prepare the corresponding Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and
Equipment (RPCPPE) following the guidelines set forth in COA Circular No. 2020-
006;
c. direct the General Services Officer to coordinate with the Municipal Accountant
and Municipal Engineer in securing the necessary documents like contracts and
disbursement payments of land acquisition and construction of infrastructure
assets as basis in recording and updating their respective PPE Property Cards
(PC);
d. require the General Services Officer to update all PCs and records based on the
latest RPCPPE; and
e. require the General Services Officer and Municipal Accountant to exert their best
efforts to reconcile their respective PPE records to come up with the reconciled
balances of the PPE accounts to be presented in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. instruct the Municipal Treasurer to prepare a certified list of taxpayers and the
amounts collectible from them and to submit it to the Municipal Accountant who
shall use it as her basis to set-up Real Property Tax and Special Education Tax
Receivables at the beginning of every year; and
b. require the Municipal Accountant to review and establish the updated correct
balances of RPT and SET Receivables, Deferred Real Property Tax and Deferred
Special Education Tax Income and the Other Deferred Credits and Fines and
Penalty accounts, taking into consideration the recording errors committed in
prior years.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to
coordinate with the Provincial Accountant to initiate the process of reconciliation of
their respective Due from and to LGU accounts, after which make adjustments in the
books of the Municipality, if necessary.

Likewise, it is recommended that the Municipal Accountant request the Provincial
Accountant to furnish her with copies of the Journal Entry Vouchers showing the amount
collected by the Province in favor of the Municipality to serve as basis in recording the Due
from LGU in the books of the Municipality as provided under Section 20 of the Manual on
NGAS for LGUs, Volume I.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to prepare a
Schedule of Unreleased Checks at the end of the year and submit it to the Municipal
Accountant who, on the basis thereof, shall prepare the JV for the restoration of cash
equivalent of the unreleased checks and recognition of the appropriate payable/liability
accounts and in addition, reverse the entries made in the ensuing year, in compliance with
Section 79 of the GAM for LGUs, Volume I.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. create a disposal team to facilitate the immediate disposal of the unserviceable
properties, including the determination of the appropriate mode of disposal,
appraisal of the same to prevent further deterioration and reduction of the
properties’ economic value; and
b. direct the Municipal Accountant to identify the subject unserviceable PPEs in the
accounting records to facilitate their derecognition from the books.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. require the Municipal Accountant to pass the necessary adjusting journal entries
for the cancellation of the stale checks and restoration of the accounts payable
following the pro-forma entries prescribed under Section 61(6) of the MNGAS,
Volume I; and
b. instruct the Municipal Treasurer to send written notices to the payees of the
existence of their checks subject for replacement one month prior to the date
when the check issued will become stale.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. instruct all Inventory Custodians and/or the General Services Officer to refrain
from issuing supplies and materials unless the request therefor is supported by a
properly accomplished Requisition and Issue Slips (RIS) submitted by the
requesting official and employee;
b. instruct the General Services Officer to prepare and submit the Summary of
Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) to the Municipal Accountant together with
the original copies of the RIS; and
c. instruct the Municipal Accountant to record Supplies and Materials Expenses
based on the SSMI and RIS submitted by the General Services Officer.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the immediate bonding of all
Inventory Custodians in compliance with the provisions of Section 101 of P.D.
No. 1445 and Section 4.2 of Treasury Circular No. 02-2009 dated August 6, 2009.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor re-establish an Inventory Committee which shall compose of adequate number of members who shall be temporarily relieved of all their regular duties to devote their full time to be able to complete the physical inventory in three months or less, in compliance with COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020; direct the Inventory Committee to conduct complete physical count of the Municipality’s PPE according to their prepared Physical Inventory Plan and prepare the corresponding Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) following the guidelines set forth in COA Circular No. 2020-006; direct the General Services Officer to coordinate with the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Engineer in securing the necessary documents like contracts and disbursement payments of land acquisition and construction of infrastructure assets as basis in recording and updating their respective PPE Property Cards (PC); require the General Services Officer to update all PCs and records based on the latest RPCPPE; and require the General Services Officer and Municipal Accountant to exert their best efforts to reconcile their respective PPE records to come up with the reconciled balances of the PPE accounts to be presented in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Re-established Inventory Committee. Inventory Committee prepared a Physical Inventory Plan. Updated some property records. Reconciliation was done but not completed.
observations.reason: The Physical Inventory Plan was not completed and the General Services Office was not able to complete the physical count due to absence of records. Updating the property records by the GSO is still ongoing due to absence of some records. The GSO was not able to conduct complete physical inventory and there was delayed submission of counted PPE to the Municipal Accountant. In addition, lack of time to conduct and complete the reconciliation. Reiterated in Part II, Observation No. 1 of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the: a. Municipal Social Welfare and Development Office to conduct a thorough validation on the eligibility of beneficiaries submitted by the Barangays to minimize the incurrence of problems during subsidy distributions; and b. OIC-Municipal Accountant to submit to the Audit Team the lacking supporting documents such as the certification from the concerned Barangay captain.
observations.managementAction: Conducted strict validation of eligibility of beneficiaries submitted by the Barangays to minimize the incurrence of problems during subsidy distributions; OIC-Municipal Accountant submitted certification of beneficiaries to barangay captain.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and other concerned municipal officials strictly comply with the guidelines governing proper recording and recognition in the books of accounts of funds received from the National Government or any other government agencies.
observations.managementAction: Ensured to be vigilant when receiving funds from National Government or any other agencies for proper recording and charging.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct all Inventory Custodians and/or the General Services Officer to refrain from issuing supplies and materials unless the request therefor is supported by a properly accomplished Requisition and Issue Slips (RIS) submitted by the requesting official and employee; instruct the General Services Officer to prepare and submit the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) to the Municipal Accountant together with the original copies of the RIS; and instruct the Municipal Accountant to record Supplies and Materials Expenses based on the SSMI and RIS submitted by the General Services Officer.
observations.managementAction: All departments prepared their own stock cards for commodities; OIC-Municipal Accountant and the Accounting Office had prepared Supplies Ledger Cards. The GSO had not prepared the RIS and SSMI and did not conduct physical count of inventories.
observations.reason: Lack of proper documentation from the GSO. The GSO was not able to provide the RIS and SSMI to the Accounting Office and did not conduct the required physical counts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and other concerned municipal officials continue to exercise diligence, prudence and proper control in the disposition of government funds by implementing cost-cutting measures and other stringent fiscal reforms and initiatives to significantly reduce, if not totally eliminate, the cash deficit of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: Observed the significant increase in the cash deficit of CY 2020 due to wrong entry in the 'Due to Other Funds' section of the Balance Sheet.
observations.reason: The increase in the cash deficit was due to wrong entry in the 'Due to Other Funds' section which substantially increases the Balance Sheet.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the MSWDO, with the assistance of the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator, to establish and maintain a GAD Database containing statistics and sex disaggregated data which are systematically gathered and regularly updated to serve as basis for gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation, in compliance with Section 4.B, Item 1 of Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 of PCW, DILG, DBM and NEDA.
observations.managementAction: Concerned municipal officials attended some trainings. Updated the socio-economic profile and completed regular updating of the GAD Database.
observations.reason: Limited face-to-face trainings due to the pandemic.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct concerned municipal officials to continue the Municipality’s effort to capacitate the personnel involved in GAD by sending them to relevant trainings and workshops, especially those that will be useful in the establishment of GAD Database. After, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the MSWDO, with the assistance of the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator, to establish and maintain a GAD Database containing statistics and sex disaggregated data which are systematically gathered and regularly updated to serve as basis for gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation, in compliance with Section 4.B, Item 1 of Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 of PCW, DILG, DBM and NEDA.
observations.managementAction: The Management promised to comply with the audit recommendations.
observations.reason: GAD Database was not yet established by the Municipality. The GAD personnel were not able to attend relevant trainings and workshops. Reiterated in Part II, Observation No. 6 of this report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor re-establish an Inventory Committee which shall compose of adequate number of members who shall be temporarily relieved of all their regular duties to devote their full time to be able to complete
the physical inventory in three months or less, in compliance with COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020. After which, it is recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Inventory Committee to prepare a Physical Inventory Plan and accordingly
conduct a comprehensive physical inventory of all PPE of the Municipality and
prepare the corresponding Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant
and Equipment (RPCPPE) following the guidelines set forth in said COA Circular;
b. OIC-General Services Officer to update the GSO’s property records based on the
RPCPPE; and
c. OIC-General Services Officer and OIC-Municipal Accountant to reconcile their
respective property records to come up with the reconciled balances of PPE
accounts to be considered as the correct balance of the PPE.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Municipal Social Welfare and Development Office to conduct a thorough
validation on the eligibility of beneficiaries submitted by the Barangays to
minimize the incurrence of problems during subsidy distributions; and
b. OIC-Municipal Accountant to submit to the Audit Team the lacking supporting
documents such as the. certification from the concerned Barangay that the
beneficiaries were in need of assistance during the pandemic and photos of all
beneficiaries or equivalent documents to prove receipt of their subsidy.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant
to strictly comply with the guidelines governing proper recording and recognition
in the books of accounts of funds received from the National Government.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
a. OIC-Municipal Accountant to record all purchases of supplies and materials
under the appropriate inventory account and to recognize expense only when
the items are already consumed based on the SSMI submitted by the GSO;
b. OIC-Municipal Accountant and the GSO to maintain SLC and SC, respectively,
for each commodity/stock;
c. GSO to prepare RIS for issuances of supplies/materials and consolidate them
on a weekly basis using the SSMI and furnish the OIC-Municipal Accountant
with copies thereof for recording in the books of the cost of issued items; and
d. GSO to conduct physical count of inventories semi-annually and to prepare
and submit the Report of the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) to the
Auditor not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and other concerned municipal
officials continue to exercise diligence, prudence and proper control in the
disposition of government funds by implementing cost-cutting measures and
other stringent but necessary fiscal reforms and initiatives to significantly reduce,
if not totally eliminate, the cash deficit of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct concerned municipal officials to
continue the Municipality’s effort to capacitate the personnel involved in GAD by
sending them to relevant trainings and workshops, especially those that will be
useful in the establishment of GAD Database. After, we recommended that the
Municipal Mayor direct the MSWDO, with the assistance of the Municipal Planning
and Development Coordinator, to establish and maintain a GAD Database
containing statistics and sex disaggregated data which are systematically
gathered and regularly updated to serve as basis for gender-responsive planning,
programming and policy formulation, in compliance with Section 4.B, Item 1 of
Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 of PCW, DILG, DBM and NEDA.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the: a. Inventory Committee to conduct a complete physical count of all the property and equipment of the Municipality to determine their existence, and the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) be submitted to the Office of the Auditor on or before January 31 of each year; b. OIC-Municipal Accountant and OIC-General Services Officer to analyze and reconcile the discrepancies in the balances of some PPE accounts between their records and make the necessary adjustments; and c. OIC-Municipal Accountant and OIC-GSO to update PPELCs, RPLCs and Property Cards and ensure that necessary information for proper monitoring and control of the assets of the Municipality are completely recorded therein.
observations.managementAction: The OIC-Municipal Accountant and OIC-General Services Officer to analyze and reconcile the discrepancies in the balances of some PPE accounts between their records and make the necessary adjustments; and OIC-Municipal Accountant and OIC-GSO to update PPELCs, RPLCs and Property Cards, respectively. The OIC-General Services Officer already started the reconciliation.
observations.reason: PPELCs, RPLCs and Property Cards were not yet updated. Reiterated in Part II, Observation No. 1 of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to analyze and investigate the cause/s of the net deficiencies of P46,021,061.89 between balances of the RPT and SET Receivables and the List of Delinquent Taxpayers as of December 31, 2019, submitted by the Municipal Treasurer’s Office, and the differences in the beginning balances and credits to RPT and SET and make the necessary adjustments in their records.
observations.managementAction: The OIC-Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer made the necessary adjustments in their records.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to prepare a request for authority to write-off the cash advances of the deceased Accountable Officer amounting to P7,000.00 in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.
observations.managementAction: A request for authority to write-off the deceased Accountable Officer amounting to P7,000.00 was submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and other concerned officials exert extra effort to generate additional source of funds to finance the operations of the Municipality and to exercise prudence and control in the disposition of government funds to ensure that expenditures to be made are within the budgetary limitations and do not exceed the total income realized during the year.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor and other concerned officials exert extra effort to generate additional source of funds to finance the operations of the Municipality and to exercise prudence and control in the disposition of government funds.
observations.reason: No net loss was incurred in CY 2020.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and other concerned municipal officials continue to exercise diligence, prudence and proper control in the disposition of government funds by implementing cost-cutting measures and other stringent but necessary fiscal reforms and initiatives to significantly reduce, if not totally eliminate, the cash deficit of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The OIC-Municipal Accountant replied that there was an error in recording the transfer of funds from the General to the Trust Fund, involving the Due to Other Funds account, which contributed to the increase in cash deficit in the Trust Fund. A correcting entry will be made to rectify the erroneous entry. The Management also assured compliance with the audit recommendation.
observations.reason: The Municipality still incurred cash deficit in the Trust Fund as of December 31, 2020 totaling P23,568,749.55. Reiterated in Part II, Observation No. 5 of this report.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendations that the Municipal Mayor coordinate and negotiate with concerned lot owners for the acquisition of the ROW to facilitate the completion of the road projects. If this is not feasible, make representation with the DA to request the realignment of the funds to other similar projects. Otherwise, it is recommended that the idle funds be returned to the DA.
observations.managementAction: Made representation with the DA to request the realignment.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the OIC-Municipal Planning and Development Officer and other concerned officials to ensure that proper planning was conducted and all issues related to project implementation are resolved before allocating funds therefor to minimize the risk of non-delivery of the projects.
observations.managementAction: Proper planning was conducted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer to ensure that all programs, projects and activities on disaster prevention, mitigation and preparedness included the MDRRM Plan are implemented accordingly.
observations.managementAction: The MDRRMO ensured that all programs, projects and activities on disaster prevention, mitigation and preparedness included the MDRRM Plan are implemented accordingly.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the LDRRMO and the OIC-MPDC to provide the necessary guidance and timetables or schedules for implementation of all the activities in the LDRRMFIP for proper monitoring of their implementation.
observations.managementAction: LDRRMFIP for CY 2019 did not provide specific timetables or schedules for implementation of projects/activities.
observations.reason: LDRRMFIP for 2019 did not provide timetables or schedules for implementation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Inventory Committee to conduct a complete physical count of all the property
and equipment of the Municipality to determine their actual existence, and the
corresponding Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment
(RPCPPE) be submitted to the Office of the Auditor on or before January 31 of
each year;
b. OIC-Municipal Accountant and OIC-General Services Officer to analyze and
reconcile the discrepancies in the balances of some PPE accounts between
their records and make the necessary adjustments; and
c. OIC-Municipal Accountant and OIC-GSO to update PPELCs, RPLCs and
Property Cards, and ensure that necessary information for proper monitoring
and control of the assets of the Municipality are completely recorded therein.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the OIC-Municipal
Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to analyze and investigate the cause/s of
the net deficiencies of P46,021,061.89 between balances of the RPT and SET
Receivables and the List of Delinquent Taxpayers as of December 31, 2019, and the
differences in the beginning balances of and credits to RPT and SET and make the
necessary adjustments in their records.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to
prepare a request for authority to write-off the cash advances of the deceased
Accountable Officer amounting to P7,000.00 in accordance with the provisions of
COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

Henceforth, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor issue a directive to the
OIC-Municipal Accountant and all concerned officials and employees to strictly
comply with the rules and regulations on the liquidation of cash advances.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and other concerned officials exert
extra effort to generate additional source of funds to finance the operations of the
Municipality and to exercise prudence and control in the disposition of government
funds to ensure that expenditures to be made are within the budgetary limitations
and do not exceed the total income realized during the year.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and other concerned municipal officials
continue to exercise diligence, prudence and proper control in the disposition of
government funds by implementing cost-cutting measures and other stringent but
necessary fiscal reforms and initiatives to significantly reduce, if not totally
eliminate, the cash deficit of the Municipality.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendations that the Municipal Mayor
coordinate and negotiate with concerned lot owners for the acquisition of the ROW
to facilitate the completion of the road projects. If this is not feasible, make
representation with the DA to request the realignment of the funds to other similar
projects. Otherwise, it is recommended that the idle funds be returned to the DA.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the OIC-Municipal Planning and
Development Officer and other concerned officials to ensure that proper planning
was conducted and all issues related to project implementation are resolved before
allocating funds therefor to minimize the risk of non-delivery of the projects.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Disaster Risk
Reduction and Management Officer to ensure that all programs, projects and
activities on disaster prevention, mitigation and preparedness included the MDRRM
Plan are implemented accordingly.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the MSWDO, with the assistance
of the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator, to establish and maintain
a GAD Database containing statistics and sex disaggregated data systematically
gathered and regularly updated to serve as basis for gender-responsive planning,
programming and policy formulation and subjected to gender analysis, in
compliance with Section 4.B, Item 1 of Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 of
PCW, DILG, DBM and NEDA.

We also recommended that the Municipality continue with its effort to capacitate
the personnel involved in the GAD Program by sending them to attend relevant
trainings and workshops.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the: a) Municipal Accountant and the OIC-General Services Officer to trace and reconcile the discrepancy of P1,555,024.51 between the Accounting records and RPCPPE and make the necessary adjustments; b) Municipal Accountant and the OIC-General Services Officer to maintain updated and complete PPELC and RPLC; and c) General Services Office to prepare and submit Report of Physical Inventory of all PPE and group them accordingly by type or category of assets to expedite reconciliation between the Accounting and their records.
observations.reason: Lack of detailed information of the previous years’ PPE.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the: a.) Municipal Accountant to book up the 14 parcels of land, which were already in the name of the Municipality, at a total market price of P46,556,097.32 and b.) Municipal Assessor and other officials concerned to facilitate the titling requirements of the 19 parcels of land in the name of the Municipality of Amadeo to establish the Municipality’s absolute ownership thereof.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant had already booked up the 14 parcels of land in 2019.
observations.reason: Not Lack of documentary requirements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to draw a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) debiting Prior Period Adjustments and crediting Loans Payable accounts by P973,336.25 to reflect the correct balances of the said affected accounts.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant had adjusted the erroneous recording of Interest expense and financial charges to reflect the correct balances of Loans Payable.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our prior year recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to: a.) Continue issuing Demand Letters to those employees with long outstanding cash advances and to those persons liable who refused to receive the Demand Letters issued to them until such time that cash advances are fully settled pursuant to 5.1.3 of COA Circular No. 97-002 and direct the withholding of salaries of the Accountable Officers who failed to liquidate their cash advances; b.) Request for authority to Write-off the cash advances of those persons accountable or with outstanding cash advances who are already dead or after due diligence, collection of which is nil, in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016; and henceforth, strictly monitor the liquidation of cash advances in compliance with the existing policies; and c.) See to it that no additional cash advance be allowed to any official/employees unless previous cash advance given to him is first settled or a proper accounting thereof is made.
observations.managementAction: The OIC-Municipal Accountant assured that she will continue to send demand letters to those with long outstanding cash advances and request authority from the Sangguniang Bayan to write-off the cash advances of the deceased accountable person.
observations.reason: No request for authority to write-off was submitted to COA.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the concerned Municipal Officials continue to find ways and means to address the cash deficit by enforcing cost-cutting measures and exercising diligence, prudence and proper control in the disposition of government funds to fully eliminate its cash deficit and be able to provide sufficient cash back up for its trust liabilities.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer and the Designated Business Permit and Licensing Officer (BPLO) commented that they are trying their best to increase their collections to eliminate cash deficit in the near future.
observations.reason: The Municipality still incurred cash deficit from last year’s P35,029,240.32 to P18,799,546.95 as of December 31, 2019 or 46.33 per cent decrease.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to exercise prudence in the use of its funds in order to be able to remit the previous years’ RPT/SET shares of the concerned LGUs in the shortest time possible for the latter to already use the subject funds. We also recommended that henceforth, shares from RPT/SET collections of the barangays and province be regularly remitted within the prescribed period as provided under Section 271 of RA 7160 and Section 38 of COA Circular No. 92-382 dated July 2, 1992.
observations.managementAction: Current year’s shares of the barangays and province from the RPT collections were remitted regularly.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Engineer and OIC-Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to resume immediately the construction of the access road from Barangay 1 to Barangay 8 as soon as the construction of damaged slope protection is completed. We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor coordinate with the lot owners for the acquisition of the ROW for the concreting of various roads and farm to market roads funded by the DA. If it’s not possible, make representation with the DA by requesting the use of the funds for similar projects. Otherwise, remit/return the funds to the DA, if no longer needed.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal construction of access road from Barangay 1 to Barangay 8 was already completed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the: a) Bids and Awards Committee to submit the necessary supporting documents to the Contract, together with a detailed breakdown of the ABC and the contract amount showing in sufficient detail the derivation thereof such as the number of paleros/laborers’ crew, prices of spare parts, machine shop cost and other maintenance parts used, etc., and henceforth include in the contract the vital information enumerated in COA Circular No. 2012-001 to determine the reasonableness of the contract cost; b) Municipal Contractor’s Environment and Natural Resources Officer to take steps to improve the monitoring system on the contractor’s performance and compliance to the terms and obligations stipulated in the contract; and c) Municipal Accountant to submit the lacking documents to support the relevant disbursements made and henceforth, refrain from processing claims with incomplete supporting documents, to avoid audit suspensions/disallowances thereon.
observations.managementAction: Bids and Awards Committee submitted the necessary documents. The Municipal Contractor’s performance were regularly monitored. Documents to support the relevant disbursements were submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Engineer and OIC-MPDC to ensure that engineering such as survey and site inspection/investigation are carried out sufficiently before the preparation of the plans to avoid delays in the implementation of projects; and b) Municipal Development Council and OIC-MPDC to review the projects included in the continuing unexpended appropriation and determine whether these are still feasible and should be priority projects of the Municipality. Otherwise, re-program the funds allotted for such projects including any unexpended balance of completed projects with the concurrence of the Sangguniang Bayan, for other development projects that will address the present needs of the Municipality and its constituents.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Development Council and OIC-MPDC re-programmed previous year’s unexpended balances for development projects.
observations.reason: There were two detailed projects in 2019 that were not implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Sangguniang Bayan and Municipal Mayor approve a resolution that will stop the MSSL Resort Corporation from its illegal use of the water falls/Balite Eco-Tourism Park Resort and collection of entrance fees therefor that duly belong to the Municipality. Thereafter, file appropriate charges for the illegal use of such property and prior earnings for its own financial gain.
observations.managementAction: The Sangguniang Bayan and Municipal Mayor passed and approved a resolution that stopped the MSSL Resort Corporation from its illegal use of the Balite Eco-Tourism Park Resort and collection of entrance fees therefor that duly belong to the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor capacitate the GAD personnel involved in the GAD Program, foremost the GFPS-TWG, by sending them to attend relevant trainings and workshops and thereafter, direct the immediate setting up of a GAD Database that will contain statistics and sex disaggregated data systematically gathered and regularly updated to serve as basis for gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation and subjected to gender analysis, in compliance with Section 4.B, Item 1 of Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 of PCW, DILG, DBM and NEDA.
observations.managementAction: The personnel involved in the GAD Program had attended the relevant trainings.
observations.reason: GAD Database was not yet established by the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor provide the necessary support in the strict implementation of source segregation and expedite the construction of a centralized Materials Recovery Facility (MRF) and comply with all other prescribed procedures and guidelines under RA No. 9003.
observations.managementAction: Construction of the centralized MRF and recovery facility (MRF) has already been completed. The waste segregation at source was implemented by the Municipality.

We reiterated our prior year’s audit recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant and the General Services Officer to prioritize the complete reconciliation of their PPE records to ascertain the reliability, existence and correctness of the PPE accounts. observations.managementAction: The Accounting and the General Services Offices are in the process of reconciling their PPE records. The Accounting Office PPE Ledger Cards were up to December 2017 only while the GSO maintain incomplete Property Cards.
observations.reason: A discrepancy of P106,975,680.58 between the Accounting records and the Report on the Physical Count of Property , Plant, and Equipment (RPCPPE) still existed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to continue issuing Demand Letters to those employees with long outstanding cash advances and to those persons liable who refused to receive the Demand Letters issued to them until such time that cash advances are fully settled pursuant to 5.1.3 of COA Circular No. 97-002 and direct the withholding of salaries of the Accountable Officers who failed to liquidate their cash advances; Request for authority to Write-off the cash advances of those persons accountable or with outstanding cash advances who are already dead or after due diligence, collection of which is nil, in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016 and henceforth, strictly monitor the liquidation of cash advances in compliance with the existing policies; and See to it that no additional cash advance be allowed to any official/employees unless previous cash advance given to him is first settled or a proper accounting thereof is made.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant continued issuing Demand Letters to those with long outstanding cash advances and to those persons liable who refused to receive the Demand Letters issued to them. Two employees refused to receive Demand Letters. No request for authority to write-off the cash advance of the demised person accountable was filed as of year-end.
observations.reason: Long outstanding cash advances remained unliquidated.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the concerned Municipal Officials enforce cost-cutting measures as provided under DILG Circular No. 2004-130, prioritize expenditures on highly necessary projects, programs and activities, reduce the hiring of JOs to fully eliminate cash deficit and be able to remit all its obligations.
observations.managementAction: Hiring of Job Orders (JOs) has been reduced.
observations.reason: The Municipality still incurred cash deficit in the General and Trust Funds in Calendar Year (CY) 2018 totaling P35,029,240.32.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to immediately remit the above RPT/SET shares to the concerned LGUs and that henceforth, shares from RPT/SET collections of the barangays and the province be regularly remitted within the prescribed period as provided under Section 271 of RA 7160 and Section 38 of COA Circular No. 92-382.
observations.managementAction: The current year’s provincial share was remitted regularly. The barangays' shares for the months of August, October to December 2018 were remitted as of February 11, 2019. The Municipal Treasurer and the Business Permit and Licensing Officer are exerting effort to increase their collections so that previous years’ RPT/SET shares of the concerned LGUs will be totally remitted.
observations.reason: Out of the P8,341,161.31 unremitted RPT shares of the barangays, P7,769,066.68 or 93.14 per cent pertains to the years 2015 and earlier and the remaining P572,094.63 pertains to the shares for the current year covering the period from August to December 2018.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the OIC-MPDO to prepare a comprehensive MDRRMFIP with specific/detailed list of PPAs for both current and continuing appropriations in compliance with Section 5.1.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-002. We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal DRRMO and other implementing offices to maximize the utilization of both the current and prior years’ fund allocation for MDRRM programs, plans and activities by seeing to it that the MDRRMFIP are properly prepared and are planned/scheduled, and to submit the Monthly Report on the Sources and Utilization of the Funds, certified by the Municipal Accountant to facilitate proper monitoring and evaluation of the agency’s utilization of the MDRRMF.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant submitted the Monthly Report on the Sources and Utilization of the Funds. The Municipal DRRMO and the other concerned implementing offices were directed to properly prepare and implement the MDRRMFIP.
observations.reason: The MDRRMFIP did not provide specific list of PPAs; out of the P11,462,486.80 only P598,040.94 or 5.22 per cent was utilized.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor discontinue allowing the special grant of cash advances to DepEd principal and teachers and see to it that cash advances are granted only to designated disbursing officer of the Municipality. We also recommended that the principal and teachers be required to immediately liquidate their outstanding cash advances in adherence to the above cited regulations.
observations.managementAction: Granting of Cash Advance to DepEd teachers and principal was discontinued.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our prior years audit recommendation that the Municipal Mayor and the concerned Municipal Officials expedite the preparation of documents necessary for the transfer of ownership of the acquired property. Henceforth, for similar transactions, submit the copy of the Contract of Sale/Deed of Absolute Sale and all the required supporting documents to the Auditor within five days from perfection of the contract so that prompt technical evaluation can be conducted.
observations.managementAction: Some copies of contracts and purchase orders for the transactions were submitted to COA beyond the prescribed period.
observations.reason: Ownership of these properties are not yet transferred in the name of the Municipality.

observations.managementAction: The Sangguniang Bayan Secretary commented that such issue was already presented to the Sanggunian Bayan. The Municipality issued business permit to MSSL Resort Corporation for the swimming pool in their premises.
observations.reason: The Municipality did not pass and approve the resolution to stop MSSL Resort Corporation and file charges; MSSL Resort Corporation continued to operate and collect entrance fees.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated the previous year's audit recommendation that the BAC Secretary and the General Services Officer submit to COA copies of contracts and Purchase Orders together with their supporting documents within five working days from execution/issuance, respectively, to facilitate their immediate review and evaluation in accordance with the abovementioned regulations.
observations.managementAction: Some copies of contracts and purchase orders and supporting documents were submitted to COA beyond the prescribed period.

Take appropriate legal actions against them for failure to settle their accounts. Hereafter, ensure strict compliance with the above regulations on the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advances, particularly the period within which to liquidate the same.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor had instructed the Municipal Accountant to continue issuing Demand Letters and prepare a request for authority to Write-off the cash advance of the demised person accountable.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor prioritize expenditures on highly necessary projects, programs and activities, reduce the hiring of JOs, use gasoline only for official purpose and limit attendance to trainings/seminars that directly related to the 10-point agenda of the government to eliminate cash deficit/overdraft.
observations.managementAction: Efforts were made by Management to decrease the cash deficit from cash overdraft P48,186,292.62 in CY 2016 to P43,639,160.26 in CY 2017. The Municipality made efforts to implement cost-cutting measures.
observations.reason: The Municipality was unable to adopt comprehensive cost-cutting/austerity measures; expenses such as hiring of Job Order personnel, training expenses and gasoline use remained significant.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer the full remittance of the above RPT/SET shares to the concerned LGUs in order to prevent its use for other purpose and for the immediate appropriation of the funds for projects/programs beneficial to the Province and Barangays. Henceforth, see to it that their shares from RPT/SET collections be regularly remitted within the prescribed period as provided under Section 271 of RA No. 7160.
observations.managementAction: Efforts were made by the Municipality to decrease the unremitted shares to the Province. The Municipality started releasing the RPT and SET shares of the Barangays and the provincial government.
observations.reason: The Municipality still failed to remit prior years’ RPT/SET shares to the concerned LGUs; only current year shares were remitted.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated the previous year’s audit recommendation that the Municipal Mayor and the concerned Municipal Officials expedite the preparation of documents necessary for the immediate transfer of ownership of the acquired property. Henceforth, for similar transactions, submit the copy of the Contract of Sale/Deed of Absolute Sale and all the required supporting documents to the Auditor within five days from perfection of the contract so that prompt technical evaluation can be conducted.
observations.managementAction: The titling of the lot in the name of the Municipality is on-going.
observations.reason: Inability of the Municipality to expedite the preparation of necessary documents for immediate transfer of ownership.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the hiring of job orders be limited to the actual manpower needs based on actual workload of each office to avoid wastage of much needed funds. Moreover, except those who are assigned in the field, all job orders be required to use the Biometric Machine for recording their daily attendance.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality was able to lessen the hiring of JOs in CY 2017. Some JOs assigned in the offices were not required to use the Biometric machine.
observations.reason: Some JOs were still hired in excess and some job orders were not required to use biometric attendance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Government strictly comply with Revenue Regulations No. 5-2011.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality complied with Revenue Regulations No. 5-2011. Compensation among those considered as “de minimis benefits” should be subjected to income/withholding.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the different end-user offices be required to prepare and submit their respective PPMP which shall consolidate the same in coordination with the Municipal Budget Officer for the funding allocation, into the consolidated APP for the approval of the Municipal Mayor and integration in the Annual Budget.
observations.managementAction: The end-user offices prepared and submitted their respective PPMP to the BAC Secretariat which shall consolidate the same in coordination with the Municipal Budget Officer for funding allocation, into the consolidated APP for the approval of the Municipal Mayor and integration in the Annual Budget.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality transact directly from suppliers of known and legitimate business establishments to obtain a fair and most advantageous price for the government. The Municipal Mayor and concerned Municipal officials should submit justifications on the basis of procuring goods to non-legitimate/reputable business establishments.
observations.managementAction: The end-user offices had transacted directly from suppliers of legitimate business establishments. The Municipality submitted justifications and documentation to support procurements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer enforce the collection of underpayment of business taxes, that the Municipal Accountant submit to the BPLO the names of the suppliers/contractors who transacted business with the Municipality including the payments made to them in the preceding calendar year to serve as basis for the computation of business taxes; and that the BPLO require the business taxpayers to submit Sworn Statements of all their gross sales/receipts for the preceding calendar year and other pertinent records/information for determination of gross receipts.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer enforced the collection of underpayment of business taxes. The Municipal Accountant submitted to the BPLO the names of the suppliers/contractors who transacted business with the Municipality. The Focal Person-BPLO required submission of documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the BAC Secretary and the General Services Officer to submit to COA copies of contracts and Purchase Orders together with their supporting documents within five working days from execution/issuance, respectively, to facilitate their immediate review and evaluation in accordance with the abovementioned regulations.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor already instructed the BAC Secretary to submit to COA copies of contracts and Purchase Orders together with their supporting documents within five working days from execution/issuance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the General Services Office responsible for accepting deliveries notify COA of the time and date of the scheduled deliveries for ocular verification of the existence and condition of the supplies, materials and equipment. In addition, copies of Inspection and Acceptance Reports should also be submitted within 24 hours upon acceptance as mandated by the regulation.
observations.managementAction: No Notice of Deliveries and Request for Inspections were received for the procurement of goods through public bidding. Copies of Inspection and Acceptance Reports were received for the procurement of goods through public bidding.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated the previous years’ audit recommendation that the Municipal Mayor reconstitute and strengthen GFPS by creating a Technical Working Group, Secretariat and GAD Management Team and define their roles and responsibilities in accordance with the provisions of JMC No. 2013-01. Through them and the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator Office set up and maintain GAD database that will serve as basis for gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor promised to create GAD Technical Working Group and maintain GAD Database.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the concerned Municipal officials demand the immediate liquidation of the outstanding cash advances of the former Municipal officials and employees. Consider sending Demand Letters to compel them to liquidate the same immediately and take legal actions against them for failure or decline to comply with the demand. Hereafter, ensure strict compliance with the above regulations on the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advances, particularly the period within which to liquidate the same.
observations.reason: The concerned Municipal officials were not able to demand/collect the overdue cash advance of the former Municipal officials.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the OIC-Municipal Treasurer the full remittance of the above RPT/SET shares to the concerned LGUs in order to prevent its use for other purpose and for the immediate appropriation of the funds for projects/programs beneficial to the Province and Barangays constituents. Henceforth, see to it that their shares from RPT/SET collections be regularly remitted within the prescribed period as provided under Section 271 of RA No. 7160.
observations.reason: The Municipality still failed to remit the prior years’ RPT/SET shares to the concerned LGUs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the BAC Secretary and the OIC-General Services Officer to submit to COA copies of contracts and purchase orders together with their supporting documents within five working days from execution/issuance, respectively, to facilitate their immediate review and evaluation in accordance with the above mentioned regulations.
observations.reason: The new designated BAC Secretary was unaware of the submission of the contracts and PO within 5 days after perfection.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our prior years’ audit recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the concerned Municipal Officials to restore the amount disbursed out of the 25% RPT shares of the barangay and immediately remit the same to the concerned barangays.
observations.reason: No actions were undertaken by Management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the concerned Municipal Officials expedite the preparation of documents necessary for the immediate transfer of ownership of the acquired property and submit to COA the lacking documents supporting its payments to avoid suspension in audit. Henceforth, for similar transactions, submit the copy of the Contract of Sale/Deed of Absolute Sale and all the required supporting documents to the Auditor within five days from perfection of the contract so that prompt technical evaluation can be conducted.
observations.reason: The transfer of the lot in the name of the Municipality is still in process.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the concerned official to refund the payment of gasoline consumption for private vehicles totalling P113,478.34. We recommended henceforth, that the Municipality discontinue the processing of claims for gasoline for private vehicles.
observations.reason: The Municipality has no government vehicle for the use of the Municipal Mayor, thus, private vehicle was used. The agency was unable to discontinue processing claims for gasoline for private vehicles due to lack of government vehicle.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the different end-user offices to prepare and submit their respective PPMP to the BAC Secretariat which shall consolidate the same in coordination with the Municipal Budget Officer for the funding allocation, into the consolidated APP for the approval of the Municipal Mayor and integration in the Annual Budget.
observations.reason: For the year 2016, the Municipality failed to submit the APP and PPMP, hence evaluation and review were not conducted.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our previous year’s audit recommendation that the Sanggunian Bayan file and approve a resolution to stop the operation of MSSL Resort Corporation for the illegal use and earnings in behalf of the government owned properties. File charges for operating without corresponding permit. Coordinate with the BIR for possible cancellation of their BIR permit due to the absence of a Municipal business permit as a requisite for its issuance.
observations.reason: The Municipality was not able to file charges against the business establishment for operating without license.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the Local School Board ensure that only projects and programs enumerated in Section 272 of RA 7160 and DECS, DBM and DILG Joint Circular No. 01-B dated June 25, 2001 are charged against the SEF. Give priority on the implementation of the construction, repair and maintenance of school buildings and facilities for the benefit of the intended beneficiaries.
observations.reason: The agency was unable to charged non related expenses to the fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Budget Officer ensure that only expenditures/projects covered under the DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 are considered in the charging against the 20% Development Fund. Moreover, strict adherence to the abovementioned guidelines on the utilization of the Development Fund should be observed.
observations.reason: Expenses totaling P118,000.00 not covered under the priority development projects still charged against the fund by the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor reconstitute and strengthen GFPS by creating a Technical Working Group, Secretariat and GAD Management Team and define their roles and responsibilities in accordance with the provisions of JMC No. 2013-01. Through them and the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator Office set up and maintain GAD database that will serve as basis for gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation.
observations.reason: The Municipality still unable to create Technical Working Group, Secretariat, GAD Management Team and GAD database during CY 2016.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant and the OIC-General Services Officer to prioritize the reconciliation of their PPE records to ascertain the reliability, existence and correctness of the Property, Plant and Equipment accounts.  Require the GSO and Accounting Office to maintain updated property cards and property ledger cards, respectively.  Moreover, direct the GSO to complete the physical inventory of all PPE.

Send Demand Letters to the concerned officials and employees for the immediate liquidation of their cash advances or take appropriate legal actions against them for failure to settle their accounts.
Hereafter, ensure strict compliance with the above regulations on the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advances, particularly the period within which to liquidate the same.
