We recommended that the Inventory Committee be directed to conduct an actual physical count of all PPE, prepare and submit the required RPCPPE in compliance with Section 124 of the NGAS Manual.
observations.managementAction: Physical Inventory was undertaken by the inventory committee but no report was submitted to the accounting office.
observations.reason: For the year 2016, the Municipality was able to conduct partial inventory of Property, Plant and Equipment. However, reconciliation of records between the General Services Office and the Accounting Office were not undertaken.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated the previous year's audit recommendation that the Municipal Mayor and Municipal officials exercise utmost diligence, prudence and proper control in the disposition of government funds. They should limit expenditures within the actual income realized during the year and make sure that obligations incurred are always supported by funds available for the purpose. Find ways to cover the cash deficit with the collections in the ensuing year.
observations.reason: The Municipality was able to lessen the cash deficit at year end.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our prior year audit recommendation that the Municipal Mayor advise the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to immediately deposit all cash on hand to date, and thereafter, deposit all collections intact and daily in accordance with Section 32 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume I, to address the exposure of government funds to losses or misappropriation.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant was able to deposit his collections intact.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the concerned BAC members to refund the excess payment of P479,056.72 and thereafter instruct the Municipal Accountant to restore the amount of P242,789.56 to the 20% DF of the Municipality.
observations.reason: The excess BAC Honoraria was partially paid by some concerned Municipal officials. A Notice of Disallowance was issued.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to remit immediately the amount of P7,643,226.86 due the BIR. Henceforth, withheld taxes due the BIR should be remitted within ten (10) days following the end of the month the withholding was made as required under Revenue Regulation No. 2-98 to prevent the incurrence of surcharges and penalties for late remittances and possible misuse or loss of funds.
observations.reason: Partial payment was made by the Municipality to the BIR of prior years’ balance of the Due to BIR.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the concerned officials strictly adhere to the provision of the aforementioned CSC Memorandum Circular by prohibiting the designation of a job order personnel as head of office. Fill up the positions by the regular employees so that continuity of service and accountability for their work performed are assured. The hiring of job orders be limited to the actual needs and should be based on necessity in order to avoid wastage of much needed funds. We recommended that all job orders be required, except those who are assigned in the field, to use the Biometric Machine for recording their daily attendance.
observations.managementAction: For CY 2016, no job order was designated as head of office. Fill up the positions by the regular employees so that continuity of service and accountability for their work performed are assured.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our prior years’ audit recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the BAC to strictly adhere and comply with Sections 32.2.1(a) and 54.1 of the RIRR of RA 9184. They should not consider incomplete bid proposals but instead declare a failure of bidding.
observations.managementAction: The BAC complied with Sections 32.2.1(a) and 54.1 of the RIRR of RA 9184.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the concerned Municipal Officials observed the limitations on the granting of cash advance for Confidential Fund as provided under DILG Memorandum Circular No. 99-65. We further recommended that the cash advance disbursement vouchers together with the transmittal letter for confidential expenses be submitted within seven (7) days after the release of check in accordance with the above-mentioned Joint Circular No. 2015-01.
observations.managementAction: Expenditure incurred for confidential undertaking already limited to the amount set under the Department of Interior and Local Government (DILG) Memorandum Circular No 99-65.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and concerned Municipal Officials to limit payments through reimbursement. Payments for supplies and materials as well as other operating expenses should be made directly to the suppliers, and deduct/withhold the mandatory taxes. Strictly adhere to the provisions of Section 93 of PD 1445 and COA Circular No. 97-002.
observations.reason: For the year 2016, the Municipality lessen and limited the procurement through reimbursement. However, some payments still processed as reimbursement.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the BAC Secretariat to post all Notices of Award, Proceed and the approved contracts for the procurement of goods, services and infrastructure projects in conformity with Sections 37.1.6 and 37.4.2 of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.
observations.managementAction: The BAC already complied with the postings of Notices of Award, Proceed and Contracts in the PhilGEPS.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to maintain Special Accounts in the General Fund and prepare financial reports with the necessary supporting schedules for economic enterprises and development projects. Accordingly, separate financial statements for the economic enterprises and development projects should be prepared at year-end to form part of the schedule of the General Fund Trial Balance as mandated under Section 313 of RA 7160 and Section 110 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume 1.
observations.reason: The Municipal accountant was only able to maintain subsidiary records for 20% Development, but, no financial statement was prepared.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the OIC-General Services Officer be advised to issue ARE of all items/units to determine and establish the accountability and responsibility of the accountable officers who have custody of the government properties. Further, for new requisitions, ARE should be prepared prior to issuance of the items to the end user.
observations.managementAction: The OIC-GSO was able to issue ARE on the procurement of properties and equipment.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the BAC Chairman require the winning bidder to post the required Performance Securities upon signing of the contracts to guarantee the faithful performance of his obligations under the contracts in line with Section 39.1 of the Revised IRR of RA 9184. We further recommended that the Notice to Proceed be issued within three (3) calendar days from the date of approval of the contract as provided under Section 37.4.1 of the Revised IRR of RA 9184.
observations.managementAction: The BAC already require the winning bidder to post the required Performance Security.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the concerned Municipal Officials discontinue making advance payment for services not yet rendered or goods not yet delivered in compliance with Section 88 of PD 1445.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality already discontinue making advance payment for services not yet rendered.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the concerned Municipal officials agreed to implement the basic requirements of the Republic Act (RA) 9003 or the Solid Waste Management Act of 2000, which are as follows: Discontinue the use of an open dumpsite for garbage disposal and instead a sanitary landfill should be developed as the final garbage disposal site. Proper segregation of solid waste specifically as to biodegradable, compostable and reusable wastes should be implemented; Enactment of ordinances by the Sangguniang Bayan that increase the awareness and cooperation of the constituents on proper solid waste management particularly on the mandatory waste segregation at source.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality already stop using open dumpsite, however, due to fund constraint; they could not develop sanitary landfill. Proper segregation of solid waste is already implemented. The Municipality conducted training/seminars to the constituents.
